programs.name: Medicaid Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)
programs.what_it_does: These programs help pay Medicare premiums, deductibles, and cost-sharing for low-income seniors; QMB covers Parts A/B premiums fully, SLMB covers Part B, QI covers Part B for higher incomes. Administered through WV Medicaid with state-specific income limits.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Medicare enrollees with low income/assets; specific limits not detailed in results but tied to Medicaid eligibility (e.g., ~$1,000-$1,500/month Part B).[1]
programs.benefit_value: QMB: Full Medicare cost-sharing (~$2,000+/year savings); SLMB/QI: Part B premium (~$185/month in 2026).

programs.name: West Virginia Aged and Disabled Waiver (ADW)
programs.what_it_does: The ADW provides home and community-based services like personal care assistance, homemaker services, skilled nursing, and non-medical transportation to help seniors live independently. Participants can self-direct by choosing their own caregivers, including family members.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Individuals aged 18+ who are elderly or disabled, meeting nursing home level of care, income up to $2,982/month, and assets $2,000.[1][3]
programs.benefit_value: Services equivalent to nursing home care costs, up to institutional limits ($5,964/month for couples); self-direction option available.[1]

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE) - Not specifically named in WV sources
programs.what_it_does: PACE provides comprehensive medical and social services to eligible seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and home care, as an alternative to nursing homes. No WV-specific name or details found in results; federal program administered locally if available.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home eligible seniors aged 55+ who can live safely in the community.
programs.benefit_value: Full capitation coverage for all Medicare/Medicaid services; value varies by location (insufficient WV data).

programs.name: SNAP (Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: SNAP provides monthly food benefits via EBT card to buy groceries, helping low-income seniors afford nutritious food. In WV, elderly (60+) have special rules for eligibility.[9]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors aged 60+ meeting income tests; SSI recipients often auto-eligible.
programs.benefit_value: Average $200-$300/month per person; max benefits based on household size/income (2026 figures vary).

programs.name: LIHEAP (Low-Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
programs.what_it_does: LIHEAP assists with heating/cooling bills and crisis fuel needs for low-income households. Administered in WV through state agencies.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors/disabled; priority for 60+ or health-vulnerable.
programs.benefit_value: $100-$1,000 annual grant per household, based on income/fuel type.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: WAP funds home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation and sealing to reduce utility costs. WV administers federally via DOE funding.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors; income <200% FPL.
programs.benefit_value: Free services worth $3,000-$7,000 per home in improvements.

programs.name: State Health Insurance Assistance Program (SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: SHIP offers free counseling on Medicare, Medicare Advantage, and savings programs. WV's program provides personalized guidance.[1]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries, especially seniors navigating options.
programs.benefit_value: Free one-on-one counseling; helps save thousands on premiums/coverage.

programs.name: Meals on Wheels
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Coordinated through WV Area Agencies on Aging.
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals.

programs.name: National Family Caregiver Support Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides respite care, training, and support to family caregivers of seniors. WV delivers via AAA.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time paid work training at community sites for low-income seniors. Federal program in WV.
programs.who_its_for: Unemployed low-income 55+ unable to find work.

programs.name: Legal Aid for Seniors (e.g., Lighthouse Program elements)
programs.what_it_does: Provides legal services for elder rights, abuse, benefits. WV has legal aid via Disability Rights and AAA.

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes/assisted living, resolving complaints. WV statewide program.
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home/assisted living residents and families.
programs.benefit_value: Free advocacy; protects rights and improves care quality.

programs.name: Lighthouse Program
programs.what_it_does: The Lighthouse Program provides in-home support services including personal care, mobility assistance, nutrition, and other help for seniors with functional needs. Services are delivered by trained caregivers after eligibility assessment by a registered nurse, with participants paying based on their monthly income.[1][2][6]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors with functional needs in their homes whose income or assets disqualify them for Medicaid services; available in every county in West Virginia.[2]
programs.whats_unique: Funded entirely by state monies from the West Virginia Bureau of Senior Services, not federal or Medicaid funds, and income-based fees make it accessible beyond standard Medicaid eligibility.[1][2][6]
programs.benefit_value: Varies by income and needs; provides personal care, mobility, nutrition support without Medicaid qualification.[2]

programs.name: FAIR (Family Alzheimer's In-Home Respite)
programs.what_it_does: FAIR offers in-home respite care to support family caregivers of individuals with Alzheimer's or related dementias. It helps caregivers by providing temporary relief through state-funded services to keep seniors at home.[6]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of seniors with Alzheimer's disease or related dementias in West Virginia.[6]
programs.whats_unique: State-specific program administered by the West Virginia Bureau of Senior Services focused exclusively on in-home respite for Alzheimer's family caregivers, beyond federal Older Americans Act services.[6]
programs.benefit_value: In-home respite hours based on medical and financial eligibility; exact value varies by assessed needs.[6]

programs.name: West Virginia Senior Legal Aid
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services to address issues faced by seniors such as housing, benefits, consumer protection, and long-term care. Operates through a state contract to support West Virginians aged 60 and over.[6][8]
programs.who_its_for: West Virginians aged 60 and older needing legal assistance.[8]
programs.whats_unique: State-contracted program via West Virginia Senior Legal Aid, Inc., tailored to WV seniors' needs like elder rights and ombudsman support, distinct from general federal legal aid.[6][8]
programs.benefit_value: Free legal consultations and representation; no monetary value specified, covers full range of senior legal issues.[8]
